expectations.text: Recommends keeping competition lifts in moderate strength-relevant rep ranges rather than going entirely high-rep
expectations.evidence: 'Start each session with a compound lift at moderate-to-high intensity (80-85% of max) for lower reps: Deadlifts: 3-4 sets × 3-5 reps.' Competition lifts stay in strength-relevant ranges.

expectations.text: Suggests a specific structure (e.g., mostly 5-10 rep range with periodic heavier work like triples)
expectations.evidence: Example session: 'Deadlifts: 4 × 4 @ 82% (strength)' followed by '4 × 6-8 (hypertrophy variation)' and '3 × 8-10.' Volume progression strategy from 12-15 to 18-24 sets over weeks.

expectations.text: Mentions an over-warm or heavy single strategy (e.g., RPE 8 single) before volume work to maintain neural readiness
expectations.evidence: Uses '4 × 4 @ 82%' as opening strength work, not singles. Does not mention an over-warm single or RPE 8 single before volume work. The expected output specifically mentioned an over-warm single around RPE 8.

expectations.text: Core message: maintain some heavy practice while accumulating hypertrophy volume
expectations.evidence: 'The key is patience—don't rush the volume increase, keep your compound lifts heavy, and prioritize recovery.' 'Keep Compound Lifts Heavy: Never let your main lifts drop below 80% intensity.'
